description: In C++, the compiler, standard library (libstdc++/libc++), and CMake version are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In C++, the compiler, standard library (libstdc++/libc++), and CMake version define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the compiler, standard library (libstdc++/libc++), and CMake version or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the compiler, standard library (libstdc++/libc++), and CMake version, then run clang-tidy, include-what-you-use, and -Wpedantic diagnostics and CMake targets (cmake --build) with the configured generator and CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical clang-tidy, include-what-you-use, and -Wpedantic diagnostics output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
